meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve Debugging Struggles
content.seo_description: Learn to master SaaS pricing while overcoming debugging hurdles. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first real-world app.
content.answer_block: To overcome debugging paralysis, treat your SaaS pricing as a feature, not an afterthought. By integrating automated pricing logic early, you force your architecture to become modular. Use Antigravity to scaffold your billing schema and Claude Code to perform iterative, test-driven debugging on your payment gateway integration.
content.intro:
The cursor blinks in the silence of your code editor, mocking the hours you have spent chasing a logic error that refuses to die. You aren't just stuck; you are hitting the wall that separates hobbyists from professional software engineers. This is the moment where most students quit, overwhelmed by the complexity of modern SaaS stacks.

But here is the secret: the complexity is a feature, not a bug. By focusing your development on the pricing architecture, you gain a structured framework that makes debugging intuitive rather than chaotic. We are going to transform your struggle into a high-value portfolio piece by leveraging AI-driven workflows to build, price, and fix your application simultaneously.

steps.title: Define the Revenue Model
steps.description: Before writing a single line of code, define your pricing tiers. This creates a mental map of your database schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Architect a multi-tenant SaaS database schema for a subscription-based product with three tiers: Hobby, Pro, and Enterprise. Include fields for usage limits and feature gating.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate prisma schema for subscription tiers'

steps.title: Scaffold the Billing Logic
steps.description: Build the integration layer. Focus on clean interfaces so that debugging becomes a matter of checking inputs versus outputs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean TypeScript interface for a payment gateway service that handles subscription upgrades and downgrades, including error handling blocks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'npm install stripe && touch services/billing.ts'

steps.title: Debug with Intent
steps.description: Use AI to isolate errors by comparing your current logic against the pricing architecture you defined in Step 1.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Identify potential race conditions in this pricing logic snippet where a user upgrades mid-cycle. Propose a robust fix.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'debug billing logic in services/billing.ts'

steps.title: Implement Unit Testing
steps.description: Automate the verification of your pricing calculations to prevent future bugs from ever reaching production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write Jest unit tests for the subscription price calculation function, covering edge cases like discount code application and tax calculation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create tests for billing.ts and execute them'

steps.title: Deploy and Monitor
steps.description: Finalize your deployment pipeline so you can monitor your pricing logic in a live environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a logging middleware that tracks pricing event errors and sends alerts to a console log during development.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'npm run build && npm start'

pro_tips: Always print your data state before and after the pricing calculation function to visualize the transformation flow.
Use feature flags to toggle pricing tiers in development without resetting your entire database.
When stuck, ask Claude Code to explain the code back to you; often, the act of hearing the logic described reveals the flaw.
Prioritize 'Fail-Fast' architecture where your app crashes immediately upon invalid price data rather than processing incorrect billing.
Document your debugging process as you go—this narrative is exactly what recruiters want to see in your portfolio.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing logic is functional, deploy your app to Vercel or Railway and share your live link in our community showcase to get peer feedback.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to build scalable, professional-grade SaaS applications that stand out to employers. Don't let the fear of a red terminal stop your progress. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
